content:
# Lithofacies

**Summary**: A rock unit or body defined on the basis of physical, chemical, and biological characteristics.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997]] used lithofacies to define the geological architecture of the reservoir.

- [[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002]] used lithofacies to categorize the reservoir units.

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] used lithofacies to distinguish between the Sulaiy and Yamama formations.

- [[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988]] characterized lithofacies to interpret the depositional environment of the Gulf of Salwa.

- [[2009-sitouah|Sitouah, 2009]] classified lithofacies to characterize the reservoir's geological diversity.

- [[2010-alroudhan|AlRoudhan, 2010]] correlated petrophysical rocks to sedimentological facies for 3D distribution.

- [[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010]] characterized lithofacies to determine the depositional model of the Lower Fadhili reservoir.

- [[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011]] used lithofacies to categorize the sedimentary environment.

- [[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011]] defined the carbonate/evaporate facies suite for the Arab-C.

- [[2012-amao|Amao, 2012]] Not stated in source

- [[2013-atig|atig, 2013]] characterized the lithofacies within the Safaniya member to identify sand bodies.

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] characterized the lithofacies of the Arab-D reservoir using an outcrop analog.

- [[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014]] classified the rocks into different lithofacies based on their sedimentological features.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] classified the Sarah Formation into 11 lithofacies based on Miall's scheme.

- [[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014]] classified the Khartam Member into four distinct lithofacies to characterize reservoir quality.

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] identified various lithofacies to characterize the sedimentological environment.

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] classified the Qusaiba Shale into three distinct lithofacies based on mineralogy and texture.

- [[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015]] quantified the lithology fraction (fractional composition) to identify sand, lime, and dolomite components.

- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] characterized the carbonate lithofacies in the Musayr Formation.

- [[2015-jabir|Jabir, 2015]] analyzed lithofacies to determine the impact of heterogeneity on reservoir quality.

- [[2015-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2015]] characterized lithofacies to determine the impact of sedimentary environment on geomechanics.

- [[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015]] classified lithofacies to characterize the heterogeneity of the Ulayyah reservoir equivalent.

- [[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015]] identified four lithofacies in the Otu field based on GR log motifs.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] identified 17 distinct lithofacies in the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] classified the Sharawra Formation into 9 distinct lithofacies (LF1-LF9).

- [[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]] used lithofacies as the primary framework for characterizing reservoir properties.

- [[2016-babker|Babker, 2016]] characterized the lithology of the Sarah paleochannel.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] identified various lithofacies to characterize the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-awad|Awad, 2016]] identified lithofacies to determine the building blocks for depositional environment interpretation.

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] identified lithofacies to characterize the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2016-salih|Salih, 2016]] categorized the Dam Formation into several lithofacies using the Dunham classification system.

- [[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016]] characterized lithofacies to understand spatial distribution in the 3D model.

- [[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017]] characterized lithofacies to determine their impact on fracture distribution.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] identified six distinct lithofacies within the Tuwaiq Mountain Formation based on mineralogy and organic richness.

- [[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017]] analyzed lithofacies to determine the depositional model.

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] classified the Sarah Formation into four distinct lithofacies (FA1-FA4).

- [[2017-adam|Adam, 2017]] defined lithofacies to characterize the sedimentological environment and reservoir quality.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] classified lithofacies to determine depositional environments and reservoir quality.

- [[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017]] classified lithofacies to model reservoir heterogeneity.

- [[2017-tayyab|Tayyab, 2017]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] performed lithofacies analysis to identify sedimentary units.

- [[2018-perdana|Perdana, 2018]] identified four siliciclastic lithofacies in the Qusaiba outcrop.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] used lithofacies to categorize the Dhruma Formation units.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] classified the Dhruma Formation into 15 lithofacies types.

- [[2020-islam|Islam, 2020]] identified lithofacies in the Sarah Formation.

- [[2020-usman|Usman, 2020]] categorized the source rocks into 12 lithofacies based on Chan et al. (2020).

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] characterized lithofacies using the Dunham classification.

- [[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021]] identified lithofacies to determine depositional environments.

- [[2022-khan|Khan, 2022]] identified and classified various lithofacies in the core samples.

- [[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022]] identified 12 lithofacies in the Jubaila Formation based on texture and sedimentary features.

- [[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022]] classified the rocks into three lithofacies based on texture and composition.

- [[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022]] analyzed the impact of lithofacies on pore systems and mechanical properties.

- [[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022]] identified nine sedimentary lithofacies in the Hanifa Formation.

- [[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022]] characterized the lithofacies of the carbonate outcrops.

- [[2022-babker|Babker, 2022]] characterized 10 distinct lithofacies in the Upper Khartam Member.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] characterized lithofacies to identify sedimentary environments in the Al Wajh Formation.

- [[2023-kusuma|Kusuma, 2023]] used lithofacies as a primary categorical variable for 1D and 3D modeling.

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] identified lithofacies to explain fluctuations in pyrolysis results.

- [[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of different facies within the zone.

- [[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024]] classified the rocks into five distinct lithofacies (LF1-LF5) to characterize heterogeneity.

- [[2024-yusmananto|Yusmananto, 2024]] correlated petrophysical groups with geological facies to determine reservoir quality.

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] defined nine lithofacies to categorize the Dhaiqa Basin sediments.

- [[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024]] analyzed facies to determine the depositional environment of the Marrat Formation.

- [[2025-kesumastuti|Kesumastuti, 2025]] not used

- [[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025]] analyzed lithofacies to understand the heterogeneity of the Minjur Sandstone.

- [[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992]] used lithofacies to interpret paleo-environmental conditions.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] characterized the lithofacies of the Wajid Sandstone.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997]] Identified using log cutoffs and categorized into 8 facies.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The non parametric Sequential Indicator Simulation was then used to build geological architecture of lithofacies using the results obtained from indicator kriging of lithofacies. ([[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997, p. 16]])

- Lithofacies are defined by three criteria: similar textural features, similar diagenetic histories, and similar petrophysical properties. ([[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002, p. 118]])

- The Sulaiy is made up of finer grained lime mudstone, while Yamama is calcarenitic limestone. ([[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992, p. 49]])

- The distribution of dolomitic lithofacies suggests a series of persistent paleoshoals or barriers. ([[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988, p. 99]])

- Lithofacies identification is a primary task in reservoir characterization. ([[2009-sitouah|Sitouah, 2009, p. 19]])

- The relationship of petrophysical rocks with sedimentological facies is used to distribute petrophysical rocks in three dimensions. ([[2010-alroudhan|AlRoudhan, 2010, p. 70]])

- Identified five distinct facies: upper shoreface, lower shoreface, inner lagoon, outer lagoon, and transgressive lagoon. ([[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010, p. 13]])

- Five distinct lithofacies were identified in the study area. ([[2011-al-masrahy|AL-MASRAHY, 2011, p. 48]])

- Identified 9 distinct sedimentary facies including ooid grainstones and thrombolites. ([[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011, p. 45]])

- Not stated in source ([[2012-amao|Amao, 2012]])

- Three main facies were identified: fluvial channel, bay, and marine. ([[2013-atig|atig, 2013, p. 44]])

- The study identified several lithofacies associations interpreted as a gentle slope platform. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 18]])

- Identified several facies including Sp (planar cross-bedded), St (trough cross-bedded), and ferruginous sandstone. ([[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014, p. 35]])

- Lithofacies include Sh (horizontally bedded), St (trough cross-bedded), Sm (massive), Sl (low-angle cross-bedded), Ss (scour fills), Sp (planar cross-bedded), and Sb (bioturbated). ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 76]])

- Four main facies were identified: well-sorted oolitic grainstone, cross-bedded oolitic fossiliferous grainstone, graded mudstone to packstone, and dolostone. ([[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014, p. 95]])

- Four main lithofacies associations were identified: poorly sorted intraclasts, graded mudstones, shoal complexes, and thin marl limestones. ([[2014-sami|Sami, 2014, p. 70]])

- Identified Lithofacies-I (micaceous), Lithofacies-II (clay-rich), and Lithofacies-III (siliceous). ([[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015, p. 19]])

- Lithology was quantified as a fractional log where values between 1 and 3 indicated mixtures of sand, limestone, and dolomite. ([[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015, p. 37]])

- Identified facies including stromatolite boundstone, oolitic grainstone, and foraminiferal packstone. ([[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015, p. 39]])

- Three distinct lithofacies were identified: sand, shaly-sand, and shale. ([[2015-jabir|Jabir, 2015, p. 13]])

- Identified distinct associations such as skeletal bank and tidal flat lithofacies in the Arab-D member. ([[2015-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2015, p. 77]])

- Identified 10 distinct lithofacies, including oolitic and grainstone types. ([[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015, p. 7]])

- Facies One (Shaly Sandstone) was identified as channel point bar deposits. ([[2015-sanuade|Sanuade, 2015, p. 47]])

- Lithofacies included oolitic grainstone, stromatolites, and mud-cracked shales indicating a variety of sub-environments. ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 14]])

- Lithofacies were differentiated based on grain size, sorting, and mineral content (e.g., LF1: calcareous sandstone; LF8: high iron oxide). ([[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016, p. 13]])

- The study identified 13 distinct lithofacies (D10-1 to D10-17) within the Dam Formation. ([[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016, p. 87]])

- Five distinct lithofacies were identified, including ferruginous sandstone and slumped siltstone. ([[2016-babker|Babker, 2016, p. 74]])

- Identified 10 distinct lithofacies including Gp, Gh, Gt, Gcm, Wm, and Gl. ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 97]])

- Nine lithofacies associations were detected in the three formations based on GR log motif and FMI data. ([[2016-awad|Awad, 2016, p. 19]])

- Ten distinct lithofacies were identified based on Dunham's classification scheme. ([[2016-malik|Malik, 2016, p. 55]])

- The study identified 10 distinct lithofacies (f1 to f10) within the Dam Formation. ([[2016-salih|Salih, 2016, p. 53]])

- Lithofacies were analyzed and grouped into four depositional sub-environments. ([[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016, p. 15]])

- Five distinct lithofacies were identified, including fissile shale and various sandstones. ([[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017, p. 37]])

- Lithofacies 3-6 are identified as potential unconventional reservoir targets due to low clay (<5%) and high organic matter (>3%). ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 32]])

- 17 distinct lithofacies were identified across the study area. ([[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017, p. 15]])

- Four lithofacies were identified: FA1, FA2, FA3, and FA4. ([[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017, p. 9]])

- Identified several lithofacies including LFT 1 (oolitic grainstone) and LFT 3 (recrystallized limestone). ([[2017-adam|Adam, 2017, p. 15]])

- Identified 9 distinct lithofacies (LFT1-LFT9) in the D5 and D6 members. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 86]])

- Defined several Lithofacies Associations (LFA-1 to LFA-7). ([[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017, p. 133]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2017-tayyab|Tayyab, 2017]])

- Identified four facies associations and thirteen sub-lithofacies. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 24]])

- Lithofacies Mdg was identified as clay-rich argillaceous mudstone to silica-rich argillaceous mudstone. ([[2018-perdana|Perdana, 2018, p. 45]])

- Identified several types including ooid-bearing limestone and packstone with bioclasts/peloids. ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 18]])

- Defined 15 distinct lithofacies (LFT1-LFT15) to characterize depositional environments. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 46]])

- Two main facies associations were identified: FA1 (Subglacial Diamictite) and FA2 (Glacial Outwash to fluvial braided channel). ([[2020-islam|Islam, 2020, p. 34]])

- Lithofacies 4 and 7 were identified as good to excellent source-rock lithofacies. ([[2020-usman|Usman, 2020, p. 60]])

- Identified eight lithofacies in the Upper Jurassic Arab-D reservoir. ([[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021, p. 29]])

- Identified several facies including LA, MS, FS, PLS, SSD, CP, and HAPS. ([[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021, p. 45]])

- Identified 13 distinct lithofacies including oolitic grainstone and quartz sandstone. ([[2022-khan|Khan, 2022, p. 51]])

- Twelve distinct lithofacies were identified (LF 1-12) representing a range from carbonate-rich to silica-rich mudstones. ([[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022, p. 40]])

- Lithofacies 1: Stromatoporoid packstone; Lithofacies 2: Dolomitic wackestone; Lithofacies 3: Dolomitic mudstone. ([[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022, p. 33]])

- Seven lithofacies were identified in the Qusaiba Member, interpreted as storm-wave dominated deposits. ([[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022, p. 24]])

- Nine lithofacies were grouped into four facies associations (FA1-FA4). ([[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022, p. 19]])

- Identified distinct lithofacies such as reefal carbonates and siliciclastic layers. ([[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022, p. 45]])

- Ten major lithofacies (LFA-1 to LFA-10) were identified. ([[2022-babker|Babker, 2022, p. 14]])

- Eleven distinct lithofacies (F1-F11) were identified across four subbasins. ([[2023-butt|Butt, 2023, p. 64]])

- Facies classification was performed using K-means clustering (KMC) to resolve inconsistencies in original labels. ([[2023-kusuma|Kusuma, 2023, p. 20]])

- Fluctuations in pyrolysis parameters are most likely due to lateral changes in microfacies. ([[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023, p. 13]])

- Sample 1 is rich in dolomite (73.4%) while Sample 2 is high in calcite (96.8%). ([[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023, p. 10]])

- Lithofacies LF1 is characterized as massive, sparsely bioturbated mudstone. ([[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024, p. 32]])

- Grain-dominated facies showed a 60-75% correlation with good reservoir quality. ([[2024-yusmananto|Yusmananto, 2024, p. 116]])

- Identified 9 distinct lithofacies (F1-F8) based on mineralogy, texture, and grain size. ([[2024-malik|Malik, 2024, p. 39]])

- The sediment classes were classified into a depositional facies model (terrestrial, deltaic and shoreface, offshore prodelta and carbonate lagoon). ([[2024-fahmi|Fahmi, 2024, p. 58]])

- Not stated in source. ([[2025-kesumastuti|Kesumastuti, 2025]])

- Five lithofacies classes were discerned based on their textures, porosity, and permeability. ([[2025-ahmed|Ahmed, 2025, p. 38]])

- Two distinct microfacies were identified that are used to interpret both age and paleo-environment. ([[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992, p. 18]])

- Identified distinct lithofacies in the Red and Grey units based on sedimentary structures. ([[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999, p. 32]])

## Contrasting Values and Interpretations

- [[1997-al-qassab|Al-Qassab, 1997]] 8 distinct lithofacies identified.

## Open Questions and Research Gaps


## Related Concepts
